whatWasImplemented: Vitest integration tests at src/__tests__/adversarial-files.test.ts covering VAL-FILE-004/005/016/017/022. In-memory fixture generator at e2e/fixtures/files/adversarial.ts builds minimal valid .docx / .pptx / macro-laden .docx archives via a hand-rolled zip writer using Node's built-in zlib (deflateRawSync + zlib.crc32, available in Node 22+). Tests run against real mammoth (v1.12) and real officeparser (v5.2) for the positive DOCX/PPTX extraction paths and the macro-ignore guarantee. For the zip-bomb and PDF-bomb cap assertions (VAL-FILE-016/017) the mammoth.extractRawText and pdf-parse.PDFParse.getText boundaries are intercepted via per-test overrides that return a string of length MAX_EXTRACTED_BYTES + 1 — this exercises the exact assertBelowCap defensive branch in src/lib/files/parsers.ts without committing ~200MB of per-test heap pressure to CI. Default mock behavior is pass-through; overrides cleared in beforeEach to keep tests hermetic.
discoveredIssues: Confirmed pre-existing flakiness in src/__tests__/gdpr-delete-account.test.ts beforeEach (auth.admin.deleteUser contention) under cold-start parallel load. Passes in isolation and on re-run of the full suite; not caused by this feature. No fix applied — environmental, not a defect in the tested code.

verification.testsRun: pnpm vitest run src/__tests__/adversarial-files.test.ts — 5/5 passing (2.67s in isolation, 1.17s tests). Full suite: 649 passing / 1 skipped / 79 files (49s) after a clean re-run.
